comment_text:
Back In Action

Have you ever had the ambition to become a spy agent or can also be called a spy agent? Well, if you have ever thought about becoming a spy agent, this back in action film is perfect for you to watch. 

Starting from a pair of spy agents who were assigned to deliver an important key to a CIA agent, they used a private jet to deliver the secret key, but they were trapped by someone and caused them to fight on the plane so that the plane crashed. Matt and Emily were safe, Matt was surprised by Emily who turned out to be pregnant, finally they both agreed to live their new lives and agreed to cut off their communication with those closest to them. Their new life is full of love, and they have 2 children, a girl and a boy. When their lives are safe and peaceful, their co-workers who have long broken up with them appear and remind them that they are being hunted by CIA agents and mafia gangs to take the secret key, how do they deal with this problem? While they already have children, and will their children be threatened by their old jobs? Watch the film to find out the next story. 

I'm going to rewatch it again!!! There are so many plot twists that I couldn't predict, hurry up and watch it!!!

comment_text: The usual sneering, stereotypical attitude towards Britain and the British, with disparaging talk of 'Jolly old England', bad food, not hugging because of repressed feelings', upper-class buffoons living in country estates, etc,  you get the picture - which is all utter crap by the way.  Oh, and apparently the Thames Barrier stops the Atlantic Ocean from flooding London, according to this nonsense  Really? Well, perhaps they'd like to explain that impossibility, given that the Thames flows into the North Sea, and is nowhere near the Atlantic. If you're American, I'll try to explain the childishness of such stereotypical views that Hollyweird puts forward regarding my country. They are akin to me turning up in the U.S. and expecting everyone to be wearing a 10-gallon hat, wearing a couple of six-shooters, shouting 'yee aww, howdy pardner!' Now wouldn't you find that somewhat pathetic?  It's mildly entertaining - emphasis on mildly - but it gets tiresome after a while, with Foxx and Diaz beating up hoards of baddies with ease, time after time.  If there's a follow-up, I won't be watching it. 🥱

video_title: Back in Action | Jamie Foxx, Cameron Diaz | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
Amazing movie. The dialogue is excellent. The pace doesn't stop. The scenes in London are just beautiful. It was a perfect piece of entertainment. Nice pivots with the added characters as the film progressed. Each was perfection. Well done.
Toward the end a smidgeon sappy, just a teeny tiny smidgeon. I wish the girl would have replied (you know the part) "as long as you don't karate kick, shoot, or set me on fire" as an answer to Diaz's question. And I wish the boy would have put his hand up to pause and relay how long it would be before hypothermia set in, to repeat the time until hypothermia from the distance as the clip otherwise went unlikely silent for a second. Because some of the bits with the kids lacked the film's action finesse. Kids frolic and playfight, and they would have come up with some attempted strategies, which may not have worked but were worth a try anyway. The parents could have been proud at the attempts and made disappointed remarks as they became epic fails, but, you know, kudos for trying, next time add a leg attack to it. They'll sharpen that move up when they get home. Just a small addition. Don't count out the kids. If their parents are sharp then the kids probably developed some skills to deal with them. But they weren't exploited to their fullest in a clever way. They were a bit too regular, like luggage you just ferry around. Maybe although they rarely help around the house they actually offer "to do anything to help" at the worst possible time, after they messed up the last impromptu maneuver, and the parents reply "no!". They can acknowledge they're "sending mixed parenting messages" and they'll "clarify the parameters when they get home".

Cameron stated: "I am back, all thanks to Jamie,” she told the host. She admitted to spending the last ten years of her life not paying attention to anything or accepting any offers. However, when this script was offered, she thought, “Maybe it’s time.” 

"And then I get a phone call from Jamie Foxx. And how do you say no to Jamie Foxx when he says, 'Hey, I've got this big action comedy that we can just have so much fun on. “If there's anyone I'm going to go back and spend months on end, on set, having a ball, laughing with, it would be Jamie.” 

Continuing her praise for co-star Jamie Foxx, Diaz claimed that she didn’t want to leave her family for 10 hours a day unless it was to work with “the most talented man in the entertainment business.” 

Thank you Jamie Foxx for getting Cameron Diaz out of retirement! 👏
